content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.
You can perform the following operation on the array:


Choose an index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N);
Choose a set of indices SSS of size KKK, (1‚Č§K‚Č§N,1‚Č§Sj‚Č§N)(1 \le K \le N, 1\le S_j \le N)(1‚Č§K‚Č§N,1‚Č§Sj‚Äč‚Č§N);
Set Ai=AS1‚äēAS2‚äēAS3‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēASKA_i = A_{S_1}\oplus A_{S_2}\oplus A_{S_3}\oplus \ldots \oplus A_{S_K}Ai‚Äč=AS1‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAS2‚Äč‚Äč‚äēAS3‚Äč‚Äč‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēASK‚Äč‚Äč, where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Let BBB denote the array obtained after sorting the array AAA. Your task is to convert the array AAA to array BBB using **at most** ‚Ćą3‚čÖN2‚ĆČ\lceil {\frac{3\cdot N}{2}} \rceil‚Ćą23‚čÖN‚Äč‚ĆČ operations.

You also need to print the operations performed to convert the array AAA to array BBB. If there are multiple ways to convert the array, print any.

It is guaranteed that there exists at least one possible solution.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print (2‚čÖM+1)(2\cdot M + 1)(2‚čÖM+1) lines:



On the first line, print MMM, the number of operations required to convert the array AAA to array BBB, where (0‚Č§M‚Č§‚Ćą3‚čÖN2‚ĆČ)(0\le M \le \lceil {\frac{3\cdot N}{2}} \rceil)(0‚Č§M‚Č§‚Ćą23‚čÖN‚Äč‚ĆČ).
Describe each operation in two lines:

In the first line, print two space-separated integers iii and KKK  (1‚Č§i,K‚Č§N)(1\le i,K \le N)(1‚Č§i,K‚Č§N), the chosen index and the number of elements in the set SSS.
In the second line, print KKK space-separated integers, the elements of set SSS. Note that all elements of the set SSS should be **distinct**.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600060006000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The array B=[1,2,2,2]B = [1, 2, 2, 2]B=[1,2,2,2]. We can convert the array AAA to array BBB using 333 operations in the following way:

Operation 111: Choose i=4i = 4i=4 and S={1,2,3,4}S = \{1, 2, 3, 4\}S={1,2,3,4}. Thus, we set A4=A1‚äēA2‚äēA3‚äēA4=2‚äē2‚äē1‚äē2=3A_4 = A_1\oplus A_2\oplus A_3\oplus A_4 = 2\oplus 2\oplus 1\oplus 2 = 3A4‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äēA2‚Äč‚äēA3‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč=2‚äē2‚äē1‚äē2=3. Thus, the array becomes [2,2,2,3][2, 2, 2, 3][2,2,2,3].
Operation 222: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and S={1,4}S = \{1, 4\}S={1,4}. Thus, we set A1=A1‚äēA4=2‚äē3=1A_1 = A_1\oplus A_4 = 2\oplus 3 = 1A1‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč=2‚äē3=1. Thus, the array becomes [1,2,2,3][1, 2, 2, 3][1,2,2,3].
Operation 333: Choose i=4i = 4i=4 and S={1,4}S = \{1, 4\}S={1,4}. Thus, we set A4=A1‚äēA4=1‚äē3=2A_4 = A_1\oplus A_4 = 1\oplus 3 = 2A4‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč=1‚äē3=2. Thus, the array becomes [1,2,2,2][1, 2, 2, 2][1,2,2,2].

Note that there might be other ways to convert the array as well.
Test case 222: The array B=[1,3,4,5,5]B = [1, 3, 4, 5, 5]B=[1,3,4,5,5]. We can convert the array AAA to array BBB using 222 operations in the following way:

Operation 111: Choose i=4i = 4i=4 and S={1,4}S = \{1, 4\}S={1,4}. Thus, we set A4=A1‚äēA4=1‚äē4=5A_4 = A_1\oplus A_4 = 1\oplus 4 = 5A4‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč=1‚äē4=5. Thus, the array becomes [1,3,5,5,5][1, 3, 5, 5, 5][1,3,5,5,5].
Operation 222: Choose i=3i = 3i=3 and S={1,3,4,5}S = \{1, 3, 4, 5\}S={1,3,4,5}. Thus, we set A3=A1‚äēA3‚äēA4‚äēA5=1‚äē5‚äē5‚äē5=4A_3 = A_1\oplus A_3\oplus A_4\oplus A_5 = 1\oplus 5\oplus 5\oplus 5 = 4A3‚Äč=A1‚Äč‚äēA3‚Äč‚äēA4‚Äč‚äēA5‚Äč=1‚äē5‚äē5‚äē5=4. Thus, the array becomes [1,3,4,5,5][1, 3, 4, 5, 5][1,3,4,5,5].

Note that there might be other ways to convert the array as well.
Test case 333: The array B=[2,4,5,5,6,6]B = [2, 4, 5, 5, 6, 6]B=[2,4,5,5,6,6]. Since the array is already equal to array BBB, we require 000 operations.
